untranslatable_reason: Diamond pattern detection requires multi-bar pattern recognition (identifying highest high at point A, lowest low at point B, and pattern formation geometry). This is not expressible using point-in-time indicator comparisons. The measure rule itself (target = breakout_price ± (highest_high - lowest_low)) requires pattern state tracking and cannot be computed from single-bar indicators. A pattern detection module or lookback window is needed.
translation_notes: Signal describes a reversal pattern (Diamond Top) with a measure rule for price targets. Entry conditions require identifying a complete diamond formation across multiple bars. The exit conditions are formula-based (breakout_price ± range) rather than indicator-based. Used pivot as a proxy for breakout detection, but this is insufficient for the actual diamond pattern logic. Author data: bearish success rate 69%, average decline 17%.
